name: Smoked Turkey
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the giblets and neck from the turkey and wash it thoroughly inside and out.
2. Make a paste with salt, pepper, dried thyme, and liquid smoke.
3. Rub the paste onto the breast of the turkey as best you can.
4. Apply the same paste to the outside of the turkey.
5. Place a quartered onion, several celery stalks, and a bay leaf inside the turkey cavity.
6. Fill the water pan with water, cheap white wine, lemon (cut in half), additional celery stalks, bay leaves, and peppercorns.
7. Soak wood chips (hickory is preferred) for at least an hour before smoking.
8. Smoke the turkey using a Weber smoker or similar large, airtight smoker.
9. Cook until the dark meat juices run clear when pierced with a fork, or until the leg moves easily.
10. Do not use the liquid from the water pan for flavoring as it may be too strong.
11. Allow the turkey to rest before carving.
